# Strait

> Open-source job orchestration platform. Schedule jobs, build workflow DAGs, manage retries, and monitor runs — all from one dashboard.

Strait handles the hard parts of background job execution: scheduling, retries with exponential backoff, dependency graphs, concurrency limits, and real-time monitoring. One API, five SDKs, zero vendor lock-in.

Website: https://strait.dev
App: https://app.strait.dev
Docs: https://docs.strait.dev

## What Strait Does

1. **Job orchestration** — Create, schedule, and trigger durable jobs with configurable retry strategies and concurrency limits.
2. **Workflow DAGs** — Build multi-step pipelines with five step types: job execution, human approval gates, sub-workflows, event waits, and sleep delays.
3. **13-state run lifecycle** — Every job execution follows a deterministic finite state machine from queued to completed (or failed, timed out, canceled).
4. **Smart retry** — Exponential backoff, fixed delays, and custom retry sequences with dead letter queue for permanently failed runs.
5. **Event triggers** — Pub/sub event routing to start jobs and workflows based on external signals.
6. **Real-time dashboard** — Monitor jobs, runs, workflows, schedules, logs, and events from a single interface.
7. **API keys and RBAC** — Scope access with granular permissions per API key and role-based access control.
8. **Multi-language SDKs** — TypeScript, Python, Go, Ruby, and Rust SDKs with identical architecture and authoring DSL.

## SDKs

- [TypeScript SDK](https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/typescript): Effect and Promise APIs for Node.js
- [Python SDK](https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/python): Sync and async clients for Python 3.11+
- [Go SDK](https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/go): Idiomatic Go with generics
- [Ruby SDK](https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/ruby): Faraday-based client for Ruby 3.1+
- [Rust SDK](https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/rust): Async/await with builder pattern

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install the CLI
cd apps/strait && go install ./cmd/strait

# Initialize a project
strait init

# Create and trigger a job
strait jobs create --name "process-payments" --endpoint "https://api.example.com/pay"
strait jobs trigger process-payments
```

## API Reference

- [REST API](https://docs.strait.dev/api-reference): 186 endpoints across 19 services
- [Full LLM reference](https://app.strait.dev/llms-full.txt): Complete architecture and SDK documentation
